q: These teenagers are very rude to the old man. But he is very nice to them.
a: No matter how rude those teenagers are to the old man, he is very nice to them.

q: Please don't neglect any details. They may seem unimportant.
a: No matter how unimportant the details may seem, please don't neglect them.

q: The math problems are hard. He solves them quickly.
a: No matter how hard the math problems are, he solves them quickly.

q: It may rain or freeze. I will still go shopping tomorrow.
a: No matter whether it rains or freezes, I will still go shopping tomorrow.

q: Her workload is extremely demanding. She still makes time for others.
a: No matter how demanding her workload is, she still makes time for others.

q: She doesn't feel well today. She came to the meeting on time.
a: No matter how unwell she feels today, she came to the meeting on time.

q: They spoke harshly to her. She kept calm and polite.
a: No matter how harshly they spoke to her, she kept calm and polite.

q: The rules seem unfair. We must follow them.
a: No matter how unfair the rules seem, we must follow them.

q: He is always busy. He answers my texts kindly.
a: No matter how busy he is, he answers my texts kindly.

q: She receives a lot of criticism. She continues her work.
a: No matter how much criticism she receives, she continues her work.

q: The traffic was heavy. We arrived before the deadline.
a: No matter how heavy the traffic was, we arrived before the deadline.

q: They raised their voices. She didn't lose her temper.
a: No matter how loudly they raised their voices, she didn't lose her temper.

q: You make mistakes. I'll be here to help.
a: No matter how many mistakes you make, I'll be here to help.

q: The weather is bad. He rides his bike to school.
a: No matter how bad the weather is, he rides his bike to school.

q: The room is small. It feels warm and comfortable.
a: No matter how small the room is, it feels warm and comfortable.

q: She has many tasks to do. She remains cheerful.
a: No matter how many tasks she has, she remains cheerful.

q: The students asked silly questions. The teacher answered all of them.
a: No matter how silly the students' questions were, the teacher answered all of them.

q: He works long hours. He never complains.
a: No matter how long he works, he never complains.

q: The results were disappointing. We congratulated the team.
a: No matter how disappointing the results were, we congratulated the team.

q: He earns little money. He still donates to charity.
a: No matter how little he earns, he still donates to charity.

q: The documents are incomplete. We have to submit them.
a: No matter how incomplete the documents are, we have to submit them.

q: You're tired. You should keep practicing.
a: No matter how tired you are, you should keep practicing.

q: He didn't sleep well. He stayed focused during the test.
a: No matter how poorly he slept, he stayed focused during the test.

q: The hike was exhausting. They reached the top smiling.
a: No matter how exhausting the hike was, they reached the top smiling.

q: His voice was low. Everyone listened carefully.
a: No matter how low his voice was, everyone listened carefully.

q: You try to stop her. She keeps going.
a: No matter how hard you try to stop her, she keeps going.

q: The idea is strange. It could be helpful.
a: No matter how strange the idea is, it could be helpful.

q: You explain it well. He still doesn't understand.
a: No matter how well you explain it, he still doesn't understand.

q: She's not an expert. She speaks with confidence.
a: No matter how inexperienced she is, she speaks with confidence.

q: He lives far away. He attends every family event.
a: No matter how far he lives, he attends every family event.

q: You ignore him. He keeps helping you.
a: No matter how much you ignore him, he keeps helping you.

q: The price is high. People still buy it.
a: No matter how high the price is, people still buy it.

q: He is underqualified. They respect him.
a: No matter how underqualified he is, they respect him.

q: The evidence is unclear. The report must be written.
a: No matter how unclear the evidence is, the report must be written.

q: She didn't prepare much. Her speech impressed everyone.
a: No matter how little she prepared, her speech impressed everyone.

q: You tell him the truth. He always blames others.
a: No matter how honestly you speak, he always blames others.

q: The child broke the vase. His mother didn't scold him.
a: No matter how badly the child broke the vase, his mother didn't scold him.

q: He feels nervous. He speaks on stage.
a: No matter how nervous he feels, he speaks on stage.

q: The system is flawed. We must use it.
a: No matter how flawed the system is, we must use it.

q: The project failed. She thanked everyone involved.
a: No matter how badly the project failed, she thanked everyone involved.
